data.title: Houthi rebels in Yemen: fired a missile at Israel as a warning
data.description: A military source within the Houthi rebels in Yemen stated that the Houthis launched a single missile attack on Israel early that morning as a warning signal. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Australia's New Law Faces Challenges as Teens Find Loopholes
data.description: Australia's recent legislation prohibiting under-16s from accessing apps like Instagram and TikTok is showing mixed results. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that while some parents are seeing early benefits, many teenagers are discovering ways to bypass the restrictions. The law aims to protect young users from potential online harms, but its effectiveness is being tested as tech-savvy teens exploit loopholes. The situation underscores the ongoing challenge of regulating digital platforms and ensuring compliance with age restrictions.

data.title: Suspicion Surrounds Alleged Impersonation Scam
data.description: Blockchain sleuth ZachXBT posted on X, expressing skepticism about a recent complaint involving an alleged impersonation scam. The complaint suggests that the victim may have been deceived by someone posing as a verified CookerFlips account, with all communications reportedly conducted through another defendant, Raj Suthar. ZachXBT advises caution, recommending that people wait for more substantial evidence before drawing conclusions about the situation.

data.title: U.S. Army Raises Enlistment Age Limit to 42 Amid Recruitment Challenges
data.description: The U.S. Army has increased the maximum enlistment age from 35 to 42, according to a report by ABC News. This change aligns the Army's age limit with those of the Air Force and Navy, which both have a maximum enlistment age of 41. According to Jin10, the decision has been in preparation for several months and is not related to the current conflict with Iran. Instead, it reflects a long-term effort to address ongoing recruitment shortages. The Army previously raised the enlistment age to 42 during the peak of the Iraq War in 2006, before lowering it to 35 a decade later.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Surpasses 2,000 USDT with a Narrowed 2.96% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 28, 2026, 06:18 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has crossed the 2,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 2,002.439941 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 2.96% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant attacked three times in 10 days
data.description: Iranian media reported that the Bushehr nuclear power plant in Iran was attacked on the night of March 27. No damage has been reported to the plant, and there are no immediate reports of casualties. The report noted that the plant is operational and stores large amounts of radioactive material, and an attack could trigger a serious nuclear accident. Attacks by the United States and Israel on facilities used for peaceful nuclear energy violate international law and pose a serious security threat to the region. The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) issued a statement late on March 27, saying it had received notification from Iran that the Bushehr nuclear power plant had been attacked again that day, marking the third attack on the plant in 10 days. The Iranian report also stated that the operating reactors were undamaged and there was no radiation leakage. The IAEA Director General Grossi called on all parties to exercise maximum military restraint to avoid the risk of a nuclear accident.

data.title: Iranian Military Shoots Down Enemy Cruise Missile Near Fordow, Iranian Media Reports
data.description: Iranian military forces have successfully intercepted and destroyed an enemy cruise missile near the Fordow region, according to Iranian media reports. The incident highlights ongoing tensions in the area, with military readiness remaining a priority for Iran. The Fordow site, known for its nuclear facilities, has been a focal point of international scrutiny and regional security concerns. This development underscores the volatile security environment in the region.

data.title: Senator Lummis Defends Senate Market Structure Bill's Impact on DeFi
data.description: Senator Cynthia Lummis has expressed strong support for the U.S. Senate market structure bill, emphasizing its robust protections for developers in the decentralized finance (DeFi) sector. According to NS3.AI, Lummis dismissed concerns that the bill would enforce Know Your Customer (KYC) requirements on DeFi developers. She highlighted ongoing bipartisan efforts to draft a third amendment to further refine the legislation.

data.title: Bitdeer Maintains Zero Bitcoin Holdings Amid Mining Operations
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company Bitdeer has announced that it continues to maintain zero Bitcoin holdings. According to Foresight News, the company reported a production of 146 BTC for the week, which it sold entirely during the same period.

data.title: Iran's Bushehr Nuclear Plant Attacked, Iranian Media Reports
data.description:
On March 28, Jin10 reported that Iranian media claimed the Bushehr nuclear power plant in Iran was attacked on the night of March 27. No damage to the plant or casualties have been reported. The plant, which is operational and stores a significant amount of radioactive material, could pose a severe nuclear accident risk if attacked. The report criticized the United States and Israel for targeting facilities that use nuclear energy for peaceful purposes, stating that such actions violate international law and pose a serious security threat to the region.

According to Jin10, the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) announced late on March 27 that it had been informed by Iran of the attack, marking the third such incident at the Bushehr plant in ten days. Iran's report indicated that the operational reactor was not damaged, and no radiation leaks occurred. The IAEA Director General, Rafael Grossi, urged all parties to exercise maximum military restraint to avoid the risk of a nuclear accident.

data.title: Ripple Enhances XRP Ledger Security with AI-Driven Measures
data.description: Ripple has introduced a comprehensive security upgrade for the XRP Ledger, incorporating artificial intelligence to bolster its defenses. According to NS3.AI, the initiative includes AI-assisted code scanning for every pull request and automated adversarial testing throughout the development process. Ripple's newly established red team has already identified over ten bugs, underscoring the effectiveness of these measures. The upcoming release of the XRP Ledger will prioritize bug fixes and improvements, foregoing the introduction of new features.

data.title: Circle Unfreezes ckUSDC and Additional Wallets
data.description: Circle has recently unfrozen ckUSDC (Dfinity Bridge) and two other hot wallets starting with 0x00e, according to Foresight News. Blockchain investigator ZachXBT reported this development on social media, noting that five out of the 16 hot wallets he previously mentioned have now been unfrozen. ZachXBT also indicated that once a civil case in New York is unsealed, he plans to publicly identify the plaintiff, experts, and all other involved parties.

data.title: Bitdeer maintains zero Bitcoin holdings, producing and selling 146 BTC this week.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed mining company Bitdeer disclosed its latest Bitcoin holdings data. As of the week ending March 28, the company mined 146 BTC and sold all of its output, currently maintaining a zero Bitcoin holding status.

data.title: Iran's Red Crescent Reports Extensive Damage from Recent Conflict
data.description: On March 28, according to Jin10, the Iranian Red Crescent reported that the ongoing conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran has resulted in significant damage to over 92,000 civilian facilities in Iran as of March 27. The statistics reveal that more than 71,000 residential properties and over 20,000 commercial facilities have been affected nationwide. In the capital, Tehran, approximately 30,000 residential and commercial units have suffered damage. Additionally, 600 schools have been impacted by the attacks. The rescue infrastructure has also been affected, with damage reported to 290 medical and emergency facilities, including 17 Red Crescent branches. Furthermore, 46 ambulances, 3 rescue helicopters, and 48 other operational vehicles have sustained damage.

data.title: CLARITY Act Revisions Aim to Strengthen Legal Protections for DeFi Developers
data.description: Cynthia Lummis announced that the latest revisions to the CLARITY Act are designed to offer enhanced legal protection for developers in the decentralized finance (DeFi) sector and those working on non-custodial software. According to NS3.AI, these changes aim to address some of the legal uncertainties faced by developers in these areas. However, Jake Chervinsky noted that the language concerning money transmission still requires further clarification. The revised bill is anticipated to be reviewed by the Senate Banking Committee in April.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Discusses Market Strategy Amid Uncertain Conditions
data.description:
On March 28, Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua shared insights on social media regarding his market strategy. According to BlockBeats, Yi mentioned setting a profit-taking line and maintaining a small position for easier management. He noted that the market rebound has not met expectations and that the ongoing conflict has not concluded in the short term, indicating a wait for new buying opportunities.

Given the recent market downturn, Yi's comments may suggest a bearish profit-taking approach.

data.title: US Senator: The Clarity Act will provide stronger protection for DeFi developers.
data.description: U.S. Senator Cynthia Lummis stated that the latest revisions to the Clarity Act will provide "the strongest protection" for DeFi and developers. Responding to concerns raised by crypto lawyer Jake Chervinsky regarding the developer liability clauses in the draft bill, she said that both parties have adjusted the relevant content to strengthen legal protections for developers of non-custodial software. However, Chervinsky pointed out that the current draft's definition of fund transfers could still expose some DeFi developers to liability risks, and the relevant clauses are not yet fully clear. The report states that the latest revised version of the bill has not yet been released and is expected to proceed to the Senate Banking Committee for review in April. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Brother Machi opened a new long position in HYPE with 10x leverage, and the overall position turned from profitable to loss.
data.description: According to Hyperbot data, Huang Licheng, known as "Brother Machi," opened a new long position in HYPE with 10x leverage this morning, currently holding 9,000 HYPE. In addition, he also holds a long position in Ethereum with 25x leverage (currently holding 3,975 ETH) and a long position in Bitcoin with 40x leverage (currently holding 33 BTC), with a total position value of approximately $10.442 million. His position has turned from profitable to loss, currently showing a floating loss of approximately $248,000.

data.title: Hyperbot Data Reveals High-Leverage Trades by Huang Licheng
data.description: Hyperbot data indicates that Huang Licheng has initiated a 10x leverage long position on HYPE, currently holding 9,000 HYPE tokens. According to Odaily, he also maintains a 25x leverage long position on Ethereum, holding 3,975 ETH, and a 40x leverage long position on Bitcoin, holding 33 BTC. The total value of these positions is approximately $10.442 million, which has shifted from profit to a current unrealized loss of about $248,000.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Extended U.S. Homeland Security Shutdown
data.description: Polymarket has estimated a 75% probability that the U.S. Department of Homeland Security shutdown will extend beyond 60 days. According to NS3.AI, this marks a 37% increase in just 24 hours following the House's approval of a temporary funding bill, which has now been forwarded to the Senate. The trading volume for this event contract has surpassed $1.1 million. However, the bill is not anticipated to pass in the Senate.
